hero.headline: Bring out the wild side of your team.
hero.sub: Create a room, share a 6-character code and break the ice with your team — remote or in the office. Up to 20 players, in English or Spanish.

hero.answer3: A meeting that could have been an email.

step1.title: Create the room
step1.desc: Choose the game language (English or Spanish) and get a 6-character code instantly.

step2.title: Gather the team
step2.desc: Share the code and up to 20 people join with their name from mobile or browser. No install needed.

step3.title: Play and vote
step3.desc: Each round a question card comes out, everyone plays their answer and the judge crowns the funniest.

pricing.title: Save your games. Come back anytime.
pricing.sub: You can play without an account — you just need the code. But if you sign up (free), we save your rooms, favourite decks and match history.

cta.title: Ready for the first game?
cta.sub: Free, no downloads required. Create a room and share the code.

help.q1: What is Malas Personas and how do you play?
help.a1: It's a round-based card game designed to get your team or friends laughing. Each round, one player is the judge and reveals a question card with a blank. Everyone else plays the funniest answer card they have. The judge picks their favorite answer, and that player earns a point. The first player to reach the target score set when the room was created wins.
help.q2: Do I need to install anything?
help.a2: No. Malas Personas runs directly in your browser on both mobile and desktop. The host creates a room and shares a 6-character code. Everyone else simply enters the code to join. No downloads required.
help.q3: How long does a game last?
help.a3: That's up to you. When creating a room, you choose the number of points needed to win. A typical game lasts between 15 and 30 minutes. The higher the target score, the longer the game.
help.q4: Can I play remotely or at the office?
help.a4: Yes. Everyone plays from their own device, so it works just as well over a video call as it does with everyone in the same room. It's perfect for breaking the ice in hybrid teams.
help.a5: From 2 to 20 players per room, including the judge (or dealer). When creating the room, you choose the maximum number of players. By default, it's set to 10. Once the room is full, other players will see it as full.
help.q6: How do I invite my team to a room?
help.a6: When you create a room, you'll receive a 6-character code and a shareable link. Send it via chat, email, or however you like. Anyone with the code can join by entering it along with their name—no account required.
help.a7: The room code remains active as long as the room stays open. You can keep using it until you close the room from the «My Rooms» page.
help.a8: No problem. They can rejoin using the same room code and continue where they left off. The game carries on for everyone else in the meantime, and if it was their turn to be the judge, the game continues without getting stuck.
help.a9: There are two decks: the Family-Friendly deck, with questions suitable for everyone—perfect for office team-building—and the Unfiltered deck, with much edgier content for groups that aren't easily offended. The host chooses which deck to use when creating the room.
help.q10: Can I play in Spanish and English?
help.a10: Yes. You choose the language when creating the room, and it applies to both the interface and the card content. You can also change it from the lobby before the game starts.
help.a11: Absolutely! I'd love to hear your ideas. Send them through the Contact page using the subject «Suggest Cards». I'll review every suggestion, and the best ones may end up in the game.
help.q12: Do I need to create an account?
help.a12: No. You can join a room using only the room code and your name. Creating an account is free and optional. It lets you save your rooms, favorite decks, and game history, so you don't have to enter your name every time.
help.q13: What data do you store about me?
help.a13: Only the minimum. If you play as a guest, only your name is used during the game—nothing is permanently stored in the database. If you register, I store your name, email address, and room history so you can access them later. I never sell your data.
help.q14: How do I delete my account?
help.a14: Until I add an automatic option, you'll need to send me an email through the Contact page. I'll permanently remove all your data from the database.
help.a15: Nothing. Malas Personas is 100% free: all decks, up to 20 players, and unlimited games, with no credit card required and no ads. Just pure fun and team building.
